articles.title: Trump Reviews Iran's 14-Point Proposal as Ceasefire Holds and a Nobel Laureate's Life Hangs in the Balance
articles.summary: President Trump is examining a 14-point Iranian proposal to end the war, delivered via Pakistan. A three-week ceasefire is holding, but the Strait of Hormuz remains a flashpoint. Meanwhile, imprisoned Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi is at "very high risk" after a cardiac crisis.

articles.title: Golden Tempo Wins Kentucky Derby, Making Cherie DeVaux First Female Winning Trainer
articles.summary: At 23-1 odds, Golden Tempo stormed to victory in the 152nd Kentucky Derby, making Cherie DeVaux the first woman to train a Derby winner. Jockey Jose Ortiz also won the Kentucky Oaks the day before. DeVaux embraced her role as a trailblazer for girls in racing.

articles.title: From Hormuz to Gaza: How Naval Blockades Have Shaped Modern History
articles.summary: The Strait of Hormuz blockade is choking off a fifth of the world's oil, but naval sieges are an ancient tactic. From Israel's 17-year blockade of Gaza to the Cuban Missile Crisis, here's how blockades have triggered humanitarian crises and brought the world to the brink of war.
